quotes.verbatim_quote:
What we are interested in is the application of the modern harmonic sound offered
by the "Upper Partials”.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This sentence declares the method's central concept — Upper Partials — which governs every chord form and voicing choice in the course.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
We are not dealing with rhythm (foundation) chords because rhythm and standard har-
monic progressions will come from other sources, such as the piano, bass or another
guitar.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This defines the method's explicit scope restriction: the student is not learning comping but rather upper-partial harmonic color over an assumed rhythmic foundation.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
*If these Forms are too hard to finger in this position, move them up to a higher chord such as

Eb jn) or atm
quotes.why_load_bearing: This is the chapter's sole explicit technique rule, directing students to transpose difficult voicings up the neck rather than struggle in a low position.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Try fingering these chords and familiarize yourself with them. Their movement will be
applied starting in lesson 2.
quotes.why_load_bearing: This instruction establishes that Lesson 1 is a pure vocabulary-building exercise and that harmonic movement — the method's core pedagogy — begins in the next lesson.
